description_long: This shrub consists of numerous slender, elongated green stems that radiate outward from a dense central base. The stems are topped with narrow, feathery seed heads that resemble those of wild grasses, giving the plant a wispy and airy appearance. The foliage is sparse, with most of the visual mass coming from the seed heads and thin, flexible stems. The color palette is primarily muted green with hints of tan and brown, suggesting a hardy, drought-tolerant species. The overall form is loose and open, with stems arching in various directions, creating a natural, untamed look. This type of shrub is commonly found in urban landscapes, parks, or roadside plantings where low-maintenance, ornamental grasses are desired.
description: A wispy, grass-like shrub with slender green stems and feathery tan seed heads, radiating outward in an airy, open form.
description_view_0: The shrub is seen at an angle, highlighting the outward spread of the stems and the feathery seed heads on the right side.
description_view_1: A nearly symmetrical, frontal view showing the full radial spread of the stems and the even distribution of seed heads.
description_view_2: The shrub is viewed from the opposite angle of view 0, emphasizing the leftward arch of the stems and the density at the base.
description_view_3: A side view that accentuates the vertical height of the shrub and the upright nature of the central stems.
